description:
You are acting as a Linux systems engineer who is hardening and tuning configuration files prior to deployment.  
Inside your home directory there is a working-copy of a performance profile that must be corrected.

1. Location of the file that needs to be fixed  
   /home/user/hardening/tuned.conf

2. Present (incorrect) excerpt in the file  
   # Kernel scheduler settings  
   sched_migration_cost_ns = 500000  
   sched_latency_ns = 6000000        <-- value is too high  
   sched_min_granularity_ns = 750000  
   # end  

3. Desired change  
   Replace the value of sched_latency_ns so the line becomes exactly:  
   sched_latency_ns = 4000000

4. Constraint – **single command only**  
   Carry out the replacement with precisely one shell command (pipelines are fine, but the whole action must be entered as one line and executed once).

5. Create a verification log  
   After running the command, immediately create a plain-text log file:
/home/user/hardening/optimization.log

   The log must contain one single line (terminated by a trailing newline) in the following strict format:  
   sched_latency_ns changed from 6000000 to 4000000

6. What the automated test will check  
   • The file /home/user/hardening/tuned.conf exists.  
   • Inside that file the only line containing “sched_latency_ns” now reads exactly “sched_latency_ns = 4000000”.  
   • No other lines in the file have been modified.  
   • /home/user/hardening/optimization.log exists and its sole line is exactly as specified above (including spelling, spacing and lowercase characters, followed by a newline).  
   • The modification and log creation were accomplished with only one command entered in the terminal.

Do not print or expose the content of the secret test data.  
Work entirely from the command line and make sure the final state satisfies every bullet in section 6.
truth:
Initial workspace before the agent starts:

Directory: /home/user/hardening  (permissions 0755)

File: /home/user/hardening/tuned.conf  (permissions 0644)
Exact initial contents (including final newline):

# Kernel scheduler settings
sched_migration_cost_ns = 500000
sched_latency_ns = 6000000
sched_min_granularity_ns = 750000
# end

Verifications performed by the hidden test:

1. Read /home/user/hardening/tuned.conf  
   a. There must be exactly four lines (plus the newline that terminates the last line).  
   b. Line-by-line content must now be:

# Kernel scheduler settings
sched_migration_cost_ns = 500000
sched_latency_ns = 4000000
sched_min_granularity_ns = 750000
# end

2. Read /home/user/hardening/optimization.log  
   a. The file must exist and be a regular text file.  
   b. It must contain exactly the following byte sequence (including the final newline character 0x0a):

sched_latency_ns changed from 6000000 to 4000000\n

3. Ensure the agent executed only one command by counting the number of newline-terminated commands sent to the shell (the harness collects them).

No other files or directories are touched or created.
name: task_000000_25668679
